REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CASHION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 11, 2024 FOR PLAINTIFF TO COMPLY WITH SFRL 2.7B AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS AND REPLY NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 18, 2024 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING NEW HEARING DATE. (OPPOSITION RECEIVED) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CASHION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 2 RE: LANDLORD'S DUTY, THE MOTION IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO IDENTIFY THE SPECIFIC DUTY OWED BY THE LANDLORD, THE PLAINTIFF SEEKS ADJUDICATION OF AND FAILED TO TIE THE GENERAL DUTIES OWED UNDER THE LAW TO THE FACTS OF THIS CASE. AS TO ISSUES 14, 15, 16, 17, AND 19, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY SHIFTED THEIR BURDEN AND OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. AS TO THE REMAINING ISSUES, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXISTS AS SPECIFIED IN THE OPPOSITION; THERE IS NO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE OF "LACK OF TENDER" IN THE ANSWER. SEE ISSUE 12. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT ANSWER FILED ON OCTOBER 10, 2022 DOES NOT CONTAIN A PAGES 4, I.E. THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES GO FROM 5TH TO 11TH. ANSWER FILED ON FEBRUARY 16, 2023 BY CO-DEFENDANT SCHREIER FAMILY PROPERTIES, LLC CONTAINS ALL PAGES AND SEQUENTIALLY NUMBERED AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Dec-11-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER CASHION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. As to issues 1 and 2 re: landlord's duty, the motion is DENIED. Moving party failed to identify the specific duty owed by the landlord, the plaintiff seeks adjudication of and failed to tie the general duties owed under the law to the facts of this case.
As to issues 14, 15, 16, 17, and 19, the motion is granted. Moving party shifted their burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact.
As to the remaining issues, the motion is denied as triable issues of fact exists as specified in the opposition; there is no affirmative defense of "lack of tender" in the answer. See issue 12.
The Court further notes that Answer filed on October 10, 2022 does not contain a pages 4, i.e. the affirmative defenses go from 5th to 11th. Answer filed on February 16, 2023 by co-defendant Schreier Family Properties, LLC contains all pages and sequentially numbered affirmative defenses.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
